weather.tip: Overcast start at 21°C with a 90% chance of rain today — carry an umbrella and build in extra buffer time for your commute, since a wet morning usually means slower traffic and possible Metro/bus delays.

around.headline: Elderly woman's body found stuffed in a suitcase, dumped in a lake in Bengaluru
around.summary: Police suspect murder after an elderly woman's body was discovered stuffed in a suitcase and dumped into a city lake. An investigation is underway; residents near lakes and secluded spots are advised to report unattended luggage or suspicious activity to police rather than investigating themselves.

around.headline: Bengaluru crypto influencer under ED scanner in Rs 300-crore scam
around.summary: The Enforcement Directorate is probing a Bengaluru-based crypto influencer over an alleged Rs 300-crore scam, days after a separate $35-million crypto OTC scam was uncovered in the city. Treat any crypto "influencer" promising guaranteed high returns as a red flag — stick to registered, regulated exchanges and never invest based on social-media hype alone.

around.headline: Five GBA staffers attacked while clearing footpath encroachments on Commercial Street
around.summary: Greater Bengaluru Authority staff were assaulted again today while carrying out a footpath-clearance drive, this time on Commercial Street, a day after a similar attack led to 10 arrests elsewhere. Expect these anti-encroachment drives to keep expanding to more neighbourhoods, with possible short-notice disruption to footpaths and roadside parking nearby.

around.headline: Namma Metro's annual fare hike plan under fresh scrutiny
around.summary: BMRCL's plan for an annual Metro fare revision is facing renewed scrutiny, with questions being raised over the timing and quantum of the hike. If you're a regular commuter, keep an eye on official BMRCL announcements before topping up passes in bulk.

around.headline: Over 13,000 Bengaluru voters flagged as 'permanently shifted' without verification
around.summary: MP Tejasvi Surya has raised concerns that roughly 13,000 voters in the city were marked as 'permanently shifted' from the electoral rolls without proper verification, alongside separate reports of other roll glitches. If you haven't checked your voter status recently, it's worth verifying your entry on the CEO Karnataka portal before the next revision closes.

around.headline: US strikes Iran for an 11th consecutive night; Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports
around.summary: The US-Iran conflict has entered an 11th night of strikes, and Houthi rebels have separately warned shipping companies to stay away from Saudi ports, raising fresh concerns for Gulf shipping and oil prices. Keep an eye on fuel prices in the coming weeks, and if you have family working in the Gulf, check current travel advisories.

events.name: Bengaluru Poetry Festival — 10th edition
events.note: The city's flagship poetry festival returns for its 10th year — worth watching for the schedule if you're into spoken word or literature.

events.name: Global Chess League 2026
events.note: Bengaluru features prominently as teams line up for this year's league — a good pick if you follow competitive chess.

events.name: MD Pallavi — multilingual concert
events.note: A multilingual live music performance at BIC — a solid low-key evening out.

events.name: 1500 Years of Indian Visual Storytelling — exhibition
events.note: An exhibition tracing 1,500 years of Indian visual art and storytelling — good for a gallery outing this week.

street_smart.title: You see a two-wheeler rider get hit by a car near MG Road and collapse on the road, unconscious with a visible head injury. A crowd starts gathering. What do you do first?
street_smart.based_on: Evergreen rotation topic (medical emergency response — 108, CPR basics). Today's feeds carried a murder investigation, a crypto scam probe, and civic-enforcement stories, none of which gave enough procedural detail to responsibly build a trap scenario, so this topic was rotated in — it hasn't appeared in the last 3 weeks of briefs.
street_smart.situation: You're one of the first people at the scene of a road accident. The rider is unconscious and bleeding from the head. More bystanders are arriving, but no one has called for help yet, and someone suggests just getting the person to a hospital as fast as possible.

choices.text: Call 108 (Karnataka's free ambulance service) immediately, ask bystanders to keep the crowd and traffic back, and avoid moving the victim unless there's an immediate danger like fire.
choices.feedback: Correct. 108 dispatches trained paramedics with the right equipment, and a head/neck injury victim can suffer serious spinal damage if moved incorrectly by untrained hands. Crowd and traffic control also matters — it keeps the scene safe until help arrives.

choices.text: Pick the person up and rush them to the nearest hospital yourself in a private car, since ambulances can take too long.
choices.feedback: This is exactly how a survivable injury can turn fatal — lifting and transporting a trauma victim without stabilizing the head and spine can worsen a spinal cord injury or internal bleeding. Private transport should only be a last resort if 108 genuinely cannot reach in time, and even then the person should be moved as a single rigid unit, not lifted by the limbs.

choices.text: Pour water on the person's face to try to wake them up before doing anything else.
choices.feedback: An unconscious person can't swallow safely, so water poured on or near the face risks choking or aspiration. It also delays the one call that actually matters — to 108.

choices.text: Take photos and videos of the scene 'for evidence' before helping the victim.
choices.feedback: Documentation can matter later for insurance or police records, but it should never come before calling for help. In trauma cases, every minute in the 'golden hour' after the injury affects the outcome.

takeaways: Save 108 (Karnataka's toll-free ambulance and emergency number) in your phone — it's faster and safer than improvised private transport in almost all cases.
Don't move a trauma victim unless they're in immediate danger (fire, oncoming traffic); incorrect handling can turn a survivable injury into a spinal or internal one.

India's Good Samaritan Law (2016) protects bystanders who help accident victims from legal harassment or being forced into lengthy police/court involvement — so don't hesitate to step in out of fear of 'getting involved.'

quiz.q: Which Bengaluru street was the site of today's footpath-clearance drive where five GBA staffers were attacked?
quiz.why: It's the second such attack on civic staff in as many days, as anti-encroachment drives continue to expand across the city.

quiz.q: Roughly how large is the crypto scam a Bengaluru 'influencer' is being probed for by the Enforcement Directorate, per today's reports?
quiz.why: It follows a separate $35-million crypto OTC scam uncovered in the city just days earlier — Bengaluru has seen a spate of ED crypto probes this month.

quiz.q: How many workers remain missing after a tunnel collapse at a hydropower project in India, according to today's reports?
quiz.why: 10 workers have been confirmed dead in the same collapse, with rescue operations still underway for those missing.

quiz.q: Which Indian law protects bystanders who help accident victims from legal harassment or being forced into lengthy police involvement?
quiz.why: It followed a Supreme Court directive after a public interest litigation highlighted how fear of legal hassle was stopping people from helping accident victims.

quiz.q: The Cauvery river, at the centre of a decades-long water-sharing dispute, flows from Karnataka into which other state?
quiz.why: The Cauvery Water Management Authority oversees the monthly release schedule between the two states, a recurring point of friction.
